data.title: Oil Gains as Trade Optimism Outweighs Venezuelan Supply Boost — Market Talk
data.description: Oil prices rise as trade-talk optimism appears to offset expectations of higher Venezuelan supply. In early trade, Brent crude is up 0.7% to $68.82 a barrel, while WTI is up 0.8% to $66.54 a barrel. "It looks like talks with the EU are moving in the right direction," ING analysts say. "These deals should help reduce uncertainty and also ease some of the demand concerns that have been lingering in the oil market." Meanwhile, President Trump gave Chevron the green light to resume pumping oil in Venezuela--a move that should see Venezuelan oil exports increase by more than 200,000 barrels a day and ease some tightness in the heavier crude market, according to ING. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

Oil prices rise as trade-talk optimism appears to offset expectations of higher Venezuelan supply. In early trade, Brent crude is up 0.7% to $68.82 a barrel, while WTI is up 0.8% to $66.54 a barrel. "It looks like talks with the EU are moving in the right direction," ING analysts say. "These deals should help reduce uncertainty and also ease some of the demand concerns that have been lingering in the oil market." Meanwhile, President Trump gave Chevron the green light to resume pumping oil in Venezuela--a move that should see Venezuelan oil exports increase by more than 200,000 barrels a day and ease some tightness in the heavier crude market, according to ING. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Valeo on track for biggest drop in 5 years after sales guidance cut
data.description:
** Shares in France's Valeo fall over 14% after the car parts supplier cut its 2025 sales guidance by about 1 billion euros ($1.18 billion), citing a forex impact of 750 million euros

** While at least three analysts say Valeo had a strong set of results in H1, they also point to the reduced sales guidance and a difficult global context for the auto industry

** "Although a range of factors support sequentially improved 2H performance ... we feel there is a lot of work to do against a challenging backdrop," Jefferies says

** The Paris-listed stock is at the bottom of European automobiles & parts (.SXAP) index, which falls 1.2%

** if losses hold, it will see its worst day since March 2020

($1 = 0.8511 euros)

data.title: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac Are Likely to Remain in Conservatorship, Pulte Says. The Stocks Are Flying. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Shaina Mishkin

Markets are acting like Fannie Mae's and Freddie Mac's exit from conservatorship is a sure bet, but the mortgage finance giants' future status is still uncertain .

Their common shares, which trade on OTC Markets, rose in the run-up to, and in the days after, the November 2024 presidential election on expectations that Donald Trump, if elected, would act to remove them from government conservatorship, which was on his agenda in his first term. But the administration's plan for the companies remains hazy in the second term.

Now, the stocks are having their best run in years. As of Thursday's close, Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae common shares were up about 106.7% and 161.9%, respectively, according to Dow Jones Market Data. It's Freddie Mac's best year to date performance since 2019, and Fannie Mae's best run in the same period since 2013.

The companies entered government conservatorship in the midst of the 2008 housing and mortgage-led financial crisis. It was meant to help stabilize the mortgage giants, and, by extension, the housing market. Exiting requires resolving questions with wide-ranging implications for the U.S. mortgage finance system, taxpayers, and investors.

One obstacle is finding a way to preserve the companies' implicit guarantee, or the understanding that the mortgages are tacitly backed by the government. The president has said he wants the companies to retain that backing, without which mortgage rates could rise.

"The Trump administration is looking for ways to recapitalize [Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac] in a manner that preserves the implicit guarantee, that does not alarm the financial community, and that does not push mortgage rates up," says Wedbush analyst Jay McCanless. "Any hint of trouble is going to freak people out."

In an interview last week with Barron's, Bill Pulte, the director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, which oversees Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae, said the two will "likely" remain in conservatorship, but added that the Trump administration is exploring ways to "take pieces, or some of, the companies public."

"I think it's likely that the companies will stay in conservatorship, but the president is actively looking at simultaneously potentially taking them public," he told Barron's. "That could mean, you know, a small amount, or pieces, or just ways to get additional liquidity."

Pulte said in a May CNBC interview that the administration was "studying actually potentially keeping it in conservatorship and taking it public." The White House had no comment on Pulte's latest comments.

Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac didn't respond to a request for comment.

Pulte didn't elaborate to Barron's on what bringing "pieces" of the companies public would entail. But his comments suggest that the administration's presumed intent to release the companies isn't as certain as some investors may have thought. His reiteration that both might remain under conservatorship while exploring ways to offer pieces publicly means "we need to think about how they're going to do it with this approach," says KBW analyst Bose George, who covers Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

Any potential step further into public markets "will be entirely up to the president," Pulte said to Barron's.

The companies' conservatorship is tied to the Treasury, which holds warrants for just under 80% of the companies' common stock, Barron's previously reported.

The Treasury didn't respond to Barron's request for comment. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated in May that addressing the companies' conservatorship wasn't a priority.

The stocks can move significantly from day to day, but investors will likely have to wait for clarity from Trump or the Treasury on the companies' future status. They should buckle up for a bumpy ride.

Write to Shaina Mishkin at shaina.mishkin@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 0.64% Lower
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is dropping 50 points.

Leading the losses are STMicroelectronics (-14.99%), ArcelorMittal (-1.90%) and LVMH (-1.80%).

Top gainers were Carrefour (3.62%), Dassault Systemes (1.74%) and Eurofins Scientific SE (1.09%).

data.title: Japan futures log sixth weekly rise on rain, China stimulus hopes
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures rose on Friday, locking in a sixth consecutive week of gains, supported by persistent rainfall in key producing regions and renewed optimism over China's economic stimulus measures.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for December delivery , added 1.5 yen, or 0.45%, to 332.2 yen ($2.26) per kg. The contract climbed 0.94% this week.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery rose 445 yuan, or 2.94%, to 15,585 yuan ($2,175.40) per metric ton.

  • The most active August butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) gained 360 yuan, or 2.99%, to 12,415 yuan ($1,732.92) per metric ton.

  • Strong futures performance and arbitrage buying, fuelled by a broader rally in commodity futures and bullish sentiment around China's stimulus measures, contributed to much of the recent upswing in rubber prices, said Farah Miller, founder of independent rubber-focused firm Helixtap Technologies.

  • This has overridden weak fundamentals that still persist, Farah noted, adding that many are watching how the Thailand-Cambodia border conflict could escalate, further lifting prices since Thailand is a major rubber producer.

  • Meanwhile, rainfall buoyed prices, while downstream demand is improving slowly, said Chinese financial information site Tonghuashun Information.

  • Elsewhere, the dollar eased to 147.10 yen , making yen-denominated assets less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • Oil prices rose as trade talk optimism supported the outlook for both the global economy and oil demand, outweighing news of the potential for more oil supply from Venezuela.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as it competes for market share with synthetic rubber, which is made from crude oil.

  • The front-month rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for August delivery last traded at 175 U.S. cents per kg, up 0.8%.

($1 = 147.0700 yen)

($1 = 7.1642 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Colonial falls after H1 results
data.description:
** Shares in Inmobiliaria Colonial fall 4% after the Spanish real estate group posted its first half results

** An increased leverage, a 1 billion euro ($1.17 billion) expansion into labs and openness to further international investments signal a potentially overstretched capital strategy, Jefferies says

** "The results reflect solid headline performance but conceal underlying fragilities in capital discipline and transparency," the brokerage adds

** Shares are at the bottom of Spain's IBEX 35

($1 = 0.8518 euros)

data.title: Equilibrated Venture acquires over 74 lakh shares of NBFC firm
data.description:
Equilibrated Venture Cflow Private Limited has acquired 74,70,000 equity shares of Paisalo Digital Limited on July 24, 2025, and July 25, 2025, increasing its stake in the company. This acquisition falls under Regulation 29(2) of the SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares & Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.

16.5011%

Acquisition DetailsEquilibrated Venture Cflow Private Limited, acting as the acquirer, increased its holding in Paisalo Digital Limited through open market purchases. The shares were acquired on July 24, 2025, and July 25, 2025.Company InformationThe target company, Paisalo Digital Limited, has a pre and post-acquisition equity share capital of ₹90,21,18,289 divided into 90,21,18,289 equity shares of Re. 1/- each. The acquisition was made through the open market.Regulatory Disclosure

This disclosure is made under Regulation 29(2) of the SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, indicating a significant change in shareholding.

data.title: Bharat Dynamics Receives ₹809 Crore Order from Armoured Vehicles Nigam
data.description:
Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) has received an order from M/s Armoured Vehicles Nigam Limited (AVNL) for the supply of one of the ATGMs (Anti-Tank Guided Missiles) valued at ₹809 crore (gross). The order is to be executed within a span of 3 years

Order DetailsParticulars

Details

Name of Entity Awarding the Order

Armoured Vehicles Nigam LimitedDescription

Confidential in view of National SecurityNature of Order

Receipt of orderOrder Type

DomesticExecution Time Period

3 yearsOrder Value

₹809 crore (gross)Related Party Transaction

No

Order DetailsThe order received by Bharat Dynamics Limited is for the supply of one of the Anti-Tank Guided Missiles (ATGM) from Armoured Vehicles Nigam Limited (AVNL). The total value of the order is ₹809 crore (gross), and it is a domestic order.Execution TimelineThe order is to be executed within a span of 3 years. This timeline provides a structured framework for BDL to plan and execute the supply of the ATGMs efficiently.Additional InformationBharat Dynamics Limited has confirmed that the promoter/promoter group/group companies do not have any interest in the entity that awarded the order. Additionally, the order does not fall within related party transactions.Market Impact

The receipt of this significant order is expected to positively impact Bharat Dynamics Limited, reinforcing its position as a key player in the defense sector. The successful execution of this order will further enhance the company's revenue stream and contribute to its growth trajectory.

data.title: Coforge's 2Q FY 2026 Earnings Likely to Remain Strong — Market Talk
data.description: Coforge has a promising outlook for its 2Q FY 2026 earnings, based on management commentary, Elara Securities (India) analysts say in a research report. The Indian IT services provider's deal with Sabre Corp. is likely to generate meaningful contribution from 2Q, the analysts say. Coforge also maintains its revenue target for FY 2027 of US$2 billion, with medium-term growth expected to be broad-based. Coforge continues to benefit from its strengths such as robust execution capability. The brokerage boosts its FY 2026-2027 revenue estimates by 4%-5% and raises the stock's target price to INR1,850.00 from INR1,770.00 with an unchanged accumulate rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at INR1,692.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: A & W Food Services of Canada, Loblaw Companies, Rogers Communications
data.description:
Securities analysts revised their ratings and price targets on several Canadian companies, including A & W Food Services of Canada, Loblaw Companies and Rogers Communications, on Friday.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • * A & W Food Services of Canada Inc : RBC raises target price to C$42 from C$34

  • * Loblaw Companies Ltd : National Bank of Canada raises target to C$242 from C$235

  • * Rogers Communications Inc : JP Morgan raises target price to C$59 from C$55

Following is a summary of research actions on Canadian companies reported by Reuters on Friday. Stock entries are in alphabetical order.

  • * A & W Food Services of Canada Inc : RBC raises target price to C$42 from C$34

  • * Loblaw Companies Ltd : National Bank of Canada raises target to C$242 from C$235

  • * Rogers Communications Inc : JP Morgan raises target price to C$59 from C$55

  • * Teck Resources Ltd : Jefferies cuts target price to C$60 from C$65

  • * Teck Resources Ltd : JP Morgan cuts to neutral from overweight

  • * Teck Resources Ltd : JP Morgan cuts target price to C$56 from C$63

data.title: France Consumer Confidence Edges Up
data.description:
Consumer confidence in France edged up to 89 in July 2025, from 88 in the previous two months, slightly surpassing market expectations of 88.

Households reported modest improvements in their perceptions of both their past (-23 vs -24) and future (-13 vs -14) personal financial situations.

The proportion of households who consider it an appropriate time to make major purchases remained unchanged (-26).

Meanwhile, the share of households who believe it is a good time to save rose again (18 vs 17).

Concerns about unemployment continued to ease, with the corresponding indicator falling to 54 from 58.

On the other hand, expectations of future inflation rose, as more households anticipate price acceleration over the next twelve months (-31 vs -36).

data.title: Thai-Cambodia military exchanges has escalated, Acting Thai PM says
data.description:
The ongoing conflict between Thailand and Cambodia has escalated, with the confrontation involving heavy weapons, Acting Thai PM Phumtham Wechayachai told reporters on Friday.

"The situation has intensified and could escalate into a state of war," he said.

On Thursday, after fighting broke out, Phumtham had said there hadn't been a declaration of war, describing the situation as a "clash".

data.title: Mozambique invites Russian companies to produce gas in country
data.description:
Mozambique is inviting Russian companies to produce gas in the country, Mozambican Minister of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation Maria Manuela dos Santos Lucas said, as per Interfax.

"We want to further diversify this sector further, and we have asked Minister Lavrov to support major Russian oil and gas companies entering the Mozambican market so that we can have more diversification," she said at a press conference following talks with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov.

"Italian and American companies discovered gas in Mozambique. But now we have other partners as well, for example, France's Total and ExxonMobil from the U.S., and companies associated with South Korea are also working here," she said.

The Coral Sul FLNG project operated by Italy's Eni for liquefying natural gas produced under the Area 4 concession in the Rovuma basin is already being carried out in Mozambique. A similar second project, Coral Norte FLNG, with capacity of 3.55 million tonnes per year, has been approved. The Rovuma Mozambique Venture (MRV) consortium, which includes ExxonMobil and China National Petroleum Corporation (CNPC), ENH, KOGAS and Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC), has a 70% stake in the project. A final investment decision has not yet been reached on Coral Norte FLNG.

ExxonMobil is the operator of the Rovuma LNG project with capacity of 15.2 million tpy, also involving MRV. No investment decision has been reached on this either.

TotalEnergies wants to build a 13 million tpy LNG plant onshore with the participation of ENH Rovuma Area Um, Mitsui, ONGC Videsh Ltd., Beas Rovuma Energy Mozambique Limited, BPRL Ventures Mozambique B.V. and PTTEP Mozambique Area 1 Limited (8.5%). The project was put on hold due to unrest in the country.

mrchub.com

data.title: Jupiter Fund Management's Assets Under Management Rises, Net Outflow Improves
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Jupiter Fund Management's assets under management rose over the first half of the year while net outflow improved, driven by continued momentum in key segments.

The London-listed money manager's assets under management stood at 47.1 billion pounds ($63.62 billion) as of June 30, up 4% from 45.3 billion pounds at Dec. 31. For the six months ended June 30, 2024, AUM was 51.3 billion pounds.

Net outflow was 200 million pounds for the period, compared with 3.4 billion in net outflow for the same period a year earlier.

The board declared an interim dividend of 2.1 pence a share, down from 3.2 pence a year earlier.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Remy Cointreau edges up on return to growth, guidance hike
data.description:
** Shares in Remy Cointreau edges up about 2%, paring earlier gains of more than 4%, after it beat Q1 sales expectations and raised its FY guidance

** The sales of the cognac and liqueur maker show first quarter of growth since early 2023, rising 5.7% organically to 220.8 million euros ($259.44 million) against consensus expectation of +2.3%

** Remy now sees FY current operating profit decline at mid-to-high-single-digits decline against mid-to-high-teens previously

** "The worst case on China tariffs has been averted, earnings appear to be bottoming,", Jefferies says

** Remy reduces expected tariff impact to 45 million euros from 65 million euros previously

** Another guidance upgrade is likely if U.S. tariffs are diluted down, Jefferies adds

** The stock is among best performers on France's SBF 120 index

($1 = 0.8511 euros)
